title: Millbrook NHS Trust — A Synthetic Acute Care Data Warehouse

resources.description: Patient demographics, clinical flags, and tracked activity counters. Uses Type-2 Slowly Changing Dimensions: a new row is written each time a tracked attribute changes, with valid_from / valid_to bracketing the version's lifetime.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Synthetic patient identifier (e.g. PAT_0000066). Stable across SCD versions.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Synthetic patient name. Not used analytically.

fields.name: person_gender_code
fields.description: NHS Data Dictionary gender code (integer). 1 = male, 2 = female, 9 = not specified (typical NHS convention).

fields.name: imd_decile
fields.description: Index of Multiple Deprivation decile for the patient's lower-layer super output area. 1 = most deprived, 10 = least deprived.

fields.name: primary_condition
fields.description: Broad clinical grouping that drives a patient's expected pathway (e.g. respiratory, cardiac, gastrointestinal).

fields.name: comorbidity_count
fields.description: Count of significant comorbid conditions. Used in readmission and LOS stratification.

fields.name: pathway_type
fields.description: Dominant care pathway for the patient (elective, emergency, cancer, etc.).

fields.name: clinician_requirement
fields.description: Clinician skill level required by the patient's primary condition. Currently constant at consultant in the data.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Patient lifecycle status (active, admitted, discharged, deceased). Changes drive new SCD rows.

fields.name: admission_count
fields.description: Running count of inpatient admissions for the patient at the time the version was written.

fields.name: total_spell_tariff
fields.description: Running sum of spell tariffs (£) across the patient's completed spells at the time the version was written. Currently 0.0 for every row.

fields.name: surgical_referred
fields.description: Whether the patient has ever been referred for surgery.

fields.name: cancer_referred
fields.description: Whether the patient has ever been referred on a cancer pathway.

fields.name: valid_to
fields.description: SCD-2 version end timestamp (exclusive). NULL for the current version.

fields.name: active
fields.description: Whether the patient is an active patient of the trust as of the version's valid window.

resources.description: Consultant workforce — 25 consultants spread across 5 specialty groups, with grade, capacity, and quality attributes. SCD-2 capable but the supplied data contains a single current version per consultant.

fields.name: consultant_code
fields.description: External consultant code used in NHS reporting.

fields.name: main_specialty
fields.description: NHS main specialty code (integer, e.g. 110 = gastroenterology).

fields.name: grade
fields.description: Clinical grade: consultant, registrar, or SHO.

fields.name: capacity_strain
fields.description: Ratio of demand to capacity. Higher = more stretched.

fields.name: specialty_group
fields.description: Broad specialty grouping: cardiac, respiratory, gastrointestinal, general, musculoskeletal.

fields.name: valid_to
fields.description: SCD-2 version end. Empty string for the current version.

resources.description: Hospital wards with capacity, department, and bed-day cost. 10 wards totalling 75 beds across two sites.

fields.name: ward_name
fields.description: Human-readable ward name (e.g. Jenner Ward, High Dependency Unit).

fields.name: ward_type
fields.description: Operational type: general, assessment, step_down, icu_hdu, maternity, paediatric.

fields.name: department
fields.description: Owning department: medicine, surgery, critical_care, women_children.

fields.name: total_beds
fields.description: Number of beds on the ward.

fields.name: cost_per_bed_day
fields.description: Daily cost per occupied bed (£). Used for spell cost analysis.

fields.name: valid_to
fields.description: SCD-2 version end. Empty string for the current version.

resources.description: Surgical procedure catalogue with OPCS-4 clinical codes, HRG payment codes, complexity tier, and tariff. Drives procedure cost and surgical income analyses.

fields.name: procedure_name
fields.description: Procedure name (e.g. Lung resection, Spinal decompression).

fields.name: hrg_code
fields.description: Healthcare Resource Group code that drives NHS payment.

fields.name: complexity
fields.description: Complexity tier: minor, moderate, major, complex.

fields.name: specialty_group
fields.description: Specialty that typically performs the procedure.

fields.name: valid_to
fields.description: SCD-2 version end. Empty string for the current version.

resources.description: Medication catalogue keyed by synthetic medication ID, categorised using the British National Formulary (BNF) hierarchy.

fields.name: bnf_category
fields.description: BNF chapter/section label (e.g. 4.8.1 Control of epilepsy).

fields.name: route
fields.description: Administration route: oral, iv, inhaled, sc.

fields.name: daily_cost
fields.description: Daily medication cost (£) at the standard dose.

fields.name: specialty_group
fields.description: Specialty most likely to prescribe the medication.

fields.name: valid_to
fields.description: SCD-2 version end. Empty string for the current version.

resources.description: Diagnostic test catalogue (imaging, pathology, cardiology) with cost and expected turnaround. Used with DM01 6-week wait analysis.

fields.name: test_name
fields.description: Test name (e.g. Ultrasound, Full Blood Count, MRI Scan).

fields.name: test_type
fields.description: Modality: blood, pathology, xray, ct, mri, ultrasound, cardiology, other.

fields.name: turnaround_days
fields.description: Expected days from order to result.

fields.name: valid_to
fields.description: SCD-2 version end. Empty string for the current version.

resources.description: Operating theatres and their scheduling capacity. 5 theatres covering general surgery, cardiac, orthopaedic, and obstetric use.

fields.name: theatre_name
fields.description: Theatre name (e.g. Cardiac Theatre, Main Theatre 2).

fields.name: specialty
fields.description: Primary specialty served by the theatre.

fields.name: sessions_per_day
fields.description: Scheduled surgical sessions per operational day (2–4).

fields.name: valid_to
fields.description: SCD-2 version end. Empty string for the current version.

resources.description: Emergency Department arrival events. The first event in an A&E attendance — records when a patient presents at A&E and is registered. Subsequent events in the same attendance (triage, clinical assessment) share the attendance_id.

fields.name: decision_id
fields.description: Synthetic monotonic event identifier across all fact tables. Useful for ordering events.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Event timestamp. Day-of-week shows the classic Monday-peak pattern in A&E.

fields.name: consultant_id
fields.description: Consultant who registered the arrival (typically the receiving clinician) (→ dim_consultant.id).

fields.name: attendance_id
fields.description: Attendance identifier unique to this A&E visit. Links arrival, triage, and assessment rows for the same attendance.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant arrival in current data.

resources.description: Triage assessment event within an A&E attendance. Carries the Manchester-style triage category (1–5) that drives clinical priority.

fields.name: attendance_id
fields.description: A&E attendance identifier. Matches the arrival row in fact_ed_arrival.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant triage in current data.

resources.description: Clinical assessment event, performed after triage. Carries wait_minutes — the elapsed time from arrival to this assessment — used as the operational proxy for the 4-hour A&E standard.

fields.name: wait_minutes
fields.description: Minutes from arrival to clinical assessment. Proxy for the 4-hour standard (which officially measures arrival-to-departure).

fields.name: attendance_id
fields.description: A&E attendance identifier. Matches the arrival and triage rows.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant assessment in current data.

resources.description: Inpatient admission events. A spell_id can have multiple rows here, capturing sub-events in the admission process. For length-of-stay work use the earliest timestamp per spell_id as the admission time.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Event timestamp. Use MIN(timestamp) per spell_id to get the admission time.

fields.name: spell_id
fields.description: Inpatient spell identifier. Links admission, ward assignments, medication, ICU care, and discharge for the same stay.

resources.description: Ward and bed assignments during a spell. A spell can have multiple rows as patients transfer between wards or beds.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Time the patient was assigned to this ward/bed.

fields.name: ward_id
fields.description: Ward the patient was assigned to (→ dim_ward.id).

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant admission_ward in current data.

resources.description: Medication administration events during an inpatient spell. One row per dose given, linked to the medication catalogue via medication_id.

fields.name: spell_id
fields.description: Inpatient spell during which the dose was given.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant specialty_care in current data.

resources.description: ICU / HDU escalation events during an inpatient spell. Marks a transfer into critical care for patients who deteriorate on a general ward.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Time of escalation to critical care.

fields.name: ward_id
fields.description: Critical care or HDU ward (→ dim_ward.id).

fields.name: spell_id
fields.description: Inpatient spell during which the escalation occurred.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant icu_hdu in current data.

resources.description: Inpatient discharge events. Records the end of an inpatient spell — patient discharged home, to another provider, or deceased. The earliest timestamp per spell_id is the discharge time.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Discharge event timestamp. Use MIN(timestamp) per spell_id for the discharge time.

fields.name: spell_id
fields.description: Inpatient spell identifier. Always matches a spell_id in fact_admission.

resources.description: Delayed Transfer of Care (DTOC) assessments. Marks the moment a patient is declared medically fit for discharge. The gap between this timestamp and the matching fact_discharge.timestamp is the DTOC delay.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Time the patient was declared medically fit for discharge.

fields.name: ward_id
fields.description: Ward the patient is in at the time of assessment (→ dim_ward.id).

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant medically_fit in current data.

resources.description: Outpatient referrals received by the trust — typically from a GP. First event on a referral-to-treatment pathway, keyed by pathway_id.

fields.name: consultant_id
fields.description: Consultant the referral is addressed to (→ dim_consultant.id).

fields.name: pathway_id
fields.description: Outpatient pathway identifier. Unique per referral, shared with subsequent appointments in fact_appointment_attended.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant referral_received in current data.

resources.description: Attended outpatient appointments. Linked to the originating referral via pathway_id. A single pathway can have multiple attended appointments (review, follow-up, etc.).

fields.name: consultant_id
fields.description: Consultant who saw the patient (→ dim_consultant.id).

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant attended in current data.

resources.description: Pre-operative assessment. First event on a surgical episode — links the patient to the planned procedure and opens the surgical_episode_id that joins the later surgeon-assignment and surgery-performed events.

fields.name: surgical_episode_id
fields.description: Surgical episode identifier. Links pre-op, surgeon assignment, and surgery performed.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant pre_op_procedure in current data.

resources.description: Surgeon assignment to a surgical episode, after the pre-op assessment. Only consultants of surgical grade appear as the assigned consultant.

fields.name: surgical_episode_id
fields.description: Surgical episode identifier. Matches the pre-op assessment row.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant pre_op_surgeon in current data.

resources.description: Completed surgery event. Carries the theatre it was performed in. Use with fact_pre_op_assessment (via surgical_episode_id) to recover the procedure and its tariff for surgical-income analyses.

fields.name: theatre_id
fields.description: Theatre in which the surgery was performed (→ dim_theatre.id).

fields.name: surgical_episode_id
fields.description: Surgical episode identifier. Matches the pre-op assessment row.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant surgery_performed in current data.

resources.description: Cancer pathway referral. Start of the 28-day Faster Diagnosis Standard (FDS) clock. Paired with fact_cancer_first_seen via cancer_pathway_id to measure days from referral to first consultant appointment.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Time the cancer referral was received. FDS clock start.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant referral_received in current data.

resources.description: First consultant appointment on a cancer pathway. FDS clock stop event. Not every referral has a first-seen row within the reporting window — right-censored cases remain open at the cutoff.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Time of the first appointment. FDS clock stop.

fields.name: consultant_id
fields.description: Consultant who saw the patient (→ dim_consultant.id).

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant first_seen in current data.

resources.description: Diagnostic test order event. Paired with fact_diagnostic_performed via request_id to measure turnaround and DM01 6-week compliance.

fields.name: request_id
fields.description: Request identifier. Matches the performed row.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant ordered in current data.

resources.description: Completed diagnostic test event. Match to fact_diagnostic_ordered on request_id to compute time-to-test. Not every order has a matching performed row within the window.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Time the test was performed. DM01 clock stop.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant performed in current data.

resources.description: Friends and Family Test survey responses. Each row is a single patient's rating on the standard NHS FFT 1–5 recommendation scale.

fields.name: recommendation_score
fields.description: NHS FFT score (1–5). Convention varies by survey; check the distribution before interpreting direction.

fields.name: consultant_id
fields.description: Consultant associated with the care episode being rated (→ dim_consultant.id).

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant responded in current data.

resources.description: Patient safety incidents reported on a ward during a spell. Low-volume table, useful for incident-rate analyses against ward-days.

fields.name: patient_id
fields.description: Patient involved in the incident (→ dim_patient.id).

fields.name: ward_id
fields.description: Ward on which the incident occurred (→ dim_ward.id).

fields.name: spell_id
fields.description: Inpatient spell during which the incident occurred.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant incident in current data.

resources.description: In-hospital death record. Linked to the spell the patient was in at time of death. Used for crude mortality rate and HSMR context.

fields.name: consultant_id
fields.description: Consultant of record at time of death (→ dim_consultant.id).

fields.name: spell_id
fields.description: Inpatient spell during which the death occurred.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State label. Constant deceased in current data.
